sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: PPG to Launch VISUALIZID Software for DELFLEET Evolution Commercial Vehicle Coatings in U.S. and Canada
feed.summary: PPG announced the availability of its advanced PPG VISUALIZID™ software for PPG DELFLEET® Evolution commercial vehicle coatings in the U.S. and Canada. This expansion marks a significant advancement in digital color-matching technology for commercial vehicle repairs, enabling faster and more accurate color identification and selection. The software eliminates the need for physical color tools and enhances productivity for repair professionals.

feed.title: Puma For Sale; Paramount Complains About Netflix, WBD - Lowe's Companies (NYSE:LOW), Home Depot (NYSE:HD)
feed.summary: Puma SE is reportedly attracting acquisition bids from several Asia-based buyers, including Anta Sports, Li Ning Co., and Asics Corp. Meanwhile, the $82.7 billion deal for Warner Bros. Discovery by Netflix is facing scrutiny and complaints of a rigged auction from Paramount Skydance Corp. Additional M&A updates include Marvell Technology's potential acquisition of Celestial AI and Targa Resources' purchase of Stakeholder Midstream.

feed.title: FDA panel unanimously votes against Johnson & Johnson MedTech interatrial shunt
feed.summary: An FDA advisory panel unanimously voted against granting approval to Johnson & Johnson MedTech's V-Wave Ventura interatrial shunt, citing that the risks of the device outweigh its benefits based on current data. While the device demonstrated safety and some benefits for heart failure with reduced ejection fraction (HFrEF) patients, it showed no benefit for those with heart failure with preserved ejection fraction (HFpEF). Johnson & Johnson plans to review the panel's recommendation and work with the FDA to determine next steps, as additional clinical evidence for HFrEF patients was recommended.

feed.title: Alphabet (GOOGL) Stock: Why Warren Buffett Just Bet $4 Billion on Google’s Future
feed.summary: Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway recently invested $4.3 billion in Alphabet (GOOGL) stock, acquiring 17.8 million shares in Q3 2025. This significant investment, alongside a bullish $400 price target from Pivotal Research, reflects growing confidence in Google's dominance in search, YouTube, and its burgeoning Gemini AI and Google Cloud segments. The move comes as regulatory challenges ease and Alphabet's custom AI chips promise increased profitability and market share.

feed.title: BioAge Labs stock rating upgraded by Morgan Stanley on promising BGE-102 data
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley upgraded BioAge Labs Inc (NASDAQ:BIOA) to Equalweight from Underweight and increased its price target to $12.00 after promising initial clinical data for its BGE-102 drug candidate. The drug showed robust biomarker reductions suggesting potential for cardiovascular risk reduction. BioAge Labs also announced positive interim results from its Phase 1 clinical trial of BGE-102, an oral NLRP3 inhibitor, demonstrating it was well-tolerated across various dose levels with mild to moderate adverse events.

feed.title: Fitch Affirms Rockwell Automation's IDR at 'A'; Outlook Stable
feed.summary: Fitch Ratings has affirmed Rockwell Automation's Long-Term Issuer Default Rating (IDR) and senior notes at 'A', and Short-Term IDR and CP at 'F1', with a Stable Outlook. This rating reflects Rockwell's strong market position in industrial automation, mitigated exposure to capital-spending cycles through diversified markets and recurring revenue, and robust financial flexibility. The company is expected to maintain strong cash generation, margin expansion, and sub-2x EBITDA leverage while pursuing disciplined acquisitions and organic investments.

feed.title: Why Is Moderna (MRNA) Stock Rocketing Higher Today
feed.summary: Moderna's stock jumped after a major long-term study from France confirmed its COVID-19 vaccine is safe and effective. The study, analyzing data from 28 million people, showed vaccinated individuals had a significantly lower risk of death from COVID and any cause. This positive news reassured investors about the company's key product despite the stock being down year-to-date.

feed.title: Constellation reaches agreement with US Department of Justice for acquisition of Calpine
feed.summary: Constellation Energy has reached an agreement with the U.S. Department of Justice to proceed with its $16.4 billion acquisition of Calpine Corporation, following approvals from FERC and other regulatory bodies. The deal required Constellation to divest four of Calpine's generating assets in the Mid-Atlantic region to address concerns about competition and potential price increases for consumers. This acquisition is one of the largest in the U.S. power industry, driven by surging electricity demand from AI data centers and electrification trends.

feed.title: Why Has Newmont Stock Surged 135%?
feed.summary: Newmont (NYSE:NEM) stock has surged 135% year-to-date in 2025, primarily driven by a dramatic increase in gold prices due to macroeconomic uncertainty. The company's strategic actions, including divesting non-core assets, cost-cutting, and significant debt reduction, have further amplified these gains. Newmont's strong financial health, with robust revenue growth and operating margins, positions it well for future upside if gold prices remain strong, though it remains exposed to commodity price volatility.

feed.title: HALPER SADEH LLC ENCOURAGES DEXCOM, INC. SHAREHOLDERS TO CONTACT THE FIRM TO DISCUSS THEIR RIGHTS
feed.summary: Halper Sadeh LLC is investigating whether certain officers and directors of DexCom, Inc. (NASDAQ: DXCM) breached their fiduciary duties to shareholders. The firm encourages long-term shareholders of DexCom to contact them to discuss potential rights, including seeking corporate governance reforms or the return of funds to the company. Shareholder involvement is highlighted as a way to improve company policies and enhance shareholder value.

feed.title: VICI Properties Boosts Tourism With New Dividend And Growing Las Vegas Portfolio
feed.summary: VICI Properties Inc., an experiential real estate investment trust (REIT), has announced a quarterly cash dividend of $0.45 per share, payable January 8, 2026, signaling its continued growth and commitment to investors. The company, a major owner of iconic Las Vegas resorts like Caesars Palace and MGM Grand, is expanding its diverse portfolio of 93 experiential properties across North America. VICI's strategic investments in gaming, hospitality, and leisure destinations are crucial for supporting local economies and attracting millions of visitors, reinforcing its role in the global tourism industry.

feed.title: Activist investor calls on KeyCorp to fire CEO, pursue share buybacks
feed.summary: HoldCo Asset Management, a Florida-based activist investor, is urging KeyCorp's board to fire CEO Chris Gorman and implement a "no acquisitions" policy, directing all excess capital to share buybacks. HoldCo, which owns 7.5 million shares of Key stock, claims Gorman's leadership has led to depressed stock value and diluted shareholder equity. KeyCorp responded by stating their focus is on driving shareholder value through disciplined growth, profitability, and capital return.

feed.title: Netflix and Warner Bros.: Surprisingly High Merger Price Alters Shareholder Value for Both
feed.summary: Netflix's proposed acquisition of Warner Bros. Discovery’s streaming and studios business for 25 times estimated 2026 EBITDA is considered exorbitantly high, despite the premier assets it would gain. This deal would significantly benefit Warner Bros. shareholders, but regulatory approval is highly uncertain, with potential antitrust concerns due to the merging of top streaming platforms. Consequently, Morningstar anticipates raising its fair value estimate for Warner Bros. while lowering Netflix's, projecting its shares to be substantially overvalued.

feed.title: Prudential to sell more shares in ICICI Prudential Asset's India IPO, offer to open Dec. 12
feed.summary: British insurer Prudential announced it will sell more shares than initially planned in the upcoming IPO of ICICI Prudential Asset Management Company in India. The company will now offer up to 49 million shares, a significant increase from the previous plan of 17.7 million shares. The three-day share sale is scheduled to begin on December 12, with anchor investors bidding a day earlier.

feed.title: KKR Said to Be in Talks to Acquire Arctos Stake in Sports Push
feed.summary: KKR & Co. is reportedly in discussions to acquire a majority stake in Arctos Partners, signaling a deeper push into sports investing. The buyout giant is exploring various sports-related investments, including teams, leagues, media rights, and financing opportunities. These talks are ongoing and confidential as KKR aims to expand its presence in the burgeoning sports industry.

feed.title: W. R. Berkley (NYSE: WRB) declares $1 special dividend, $776M capital return in 2025
feed.summary: W. R. Berkley Corporation announced a special cash dividend of $1.00 per share and a regular quarterly cash dividend of 9 cents per share, both payable on December 29, 2025. These dividends, combined with a previous special dividend and share repurchases, bring the total capital returned to shareholders in 2025 to approximately $776.0 million. This also increases the total special cash dividends paid in 2025 to $1.50 per share.

feed.title: Target tries to get back in the game with overnight delivery
feed.summary: Target is implementing overnight delivery to compete with Amazon and Walmart, who are also enhancing their delivery services. Target's move follows a disappointing third quarter, with the company aiming to boost sales through improved fulfillment options like curbside pickup and next-day delivery. Its competitors are rapidly innovating, with Amazon testing 30-minute grocery delivery and Walmart expanding AI-driven ordering and same-day pharmacy services.

feed.title: Custom Nightguards Initiated in Dentrix Now Available from Henry Schein
feed.summary: Henry Schein and Henry Schein One have launched custom nightguards through their LinkIt digital workflow, integrated with the Dentrix platform. This end-to-end solution streamlines the process from patient scan to final delivery, eliminating manual steps and connecting various systems. The workflow aims to enhance patient experience, reduce errors, and improve efficiency for dental practices.

feed.title: Health Rounds: Shingles vaccine reduces risk of death from dementia, study finds
feed.summary: A new study shows that individuals with dementia who received a shingles vaccine had a significantly lower risk of dying from dementia, suggesting the vaccine may slow disease progression. Researchers found Merck's Zostavax vaccine lowered the risk of dementia-related death by nearly 30 percentage points, supporting earlier findings that it also reduces the likelihood of developing dementia. Additionally, the article highlights that continuous glucose monitoring with devices from Dexcom can help women with gestational diabetes reduce the risk of having newborns with above-average birth weight.

feed.title: Evergy completes tree planting to reduce urban heat in Kansas City
feed.summary: Evergy has completed its initiative to plant 1,700 trees in Kansas City to combat urban heat islands. This tree planting is part of a broader strategy by the utility company, which also includes using reflective paint and white roofs to reduce urban temperatures. The project aims to mitigate the increased heat caused by buildings and concrete in the city's urban areas.

feed.title: │Invesco Ltd. Buys 283,321 Shares of Citizens Financial Group, Inc. $CFG
feed.summary: │Invesco Ltd. increased its stake in Citizens Financial Group, Inc. (NYSE:CFG) by 1.2% in Q2, acquiring an additional 283,321 shares and now owning 23,671,355 shares valued at $1.059 billion. Citizens Financial Group recently reported strong Q3 earnings, exceeding analyst expectations with $1.05 EPS and $2.12 billion in revenue, prompting an increase in its quarterly dividend to $0.46. Analysts have also responded positively, with several upgrading ratings and raising price targets for CFG stock, contributing to an average Street target of $56.57 and a consensus "Buy" rating.

feed.title: KMX Investor Alert: A Securities Fraud Class Action Lawsuit Has Been Filed Against CarMax, Inc. (KMX)
feed.summary: An amended securities class action lawsuit has been filed against CarMax, Inc. (KMX), expanding the class period to include investors who purchased securities between June 20, 2025, and November 5, 2025. The lawsuit alleges that CarMax overstated its growth prospects, which were temporarily boosted by customer car purchases due to tariff speculation. Investors have until January 2, 2026, to seek appointment as lead plaintiff.

feed.title: Evergy completes tree planting to reduce urban heat in Kansas City
feed.summary: Evergy has completed its tree planting initiative in Kansas City, aimed at reducing urban heat. The program successfully planted the final trees as part of its urban heat island reduction efforts. This initiative contributes to creating cooler environments within the city.

feed.title: Guidewire Supports Cincinnati Insurance to Transform Claims Operations
feed.summary: Guidewire Software, Inc. has announced The Cincinnati Insurance Company successfully deployed Guidewire ClaimCenter to modernize its claims operations, starting with workers' compensation and expanding to other lines of business. This initiative aims to enhance operational efficiency, accelerate new capabilities, and improve customer experience by leveraging the Guidewire Cloud Platform. Guidewire is experiencing increasing demand for its cloud solutions and continues to integrate advanced technologies like generative AI and analytics to boost insurer productivity and decision-making.

feed.title: Elevance Health Expands Virtual Assistant to Help Members Navigate Healthcare with Confidence
feed.summary: Elevance Health has expanded its Virtual Assistant, available through the Sydney Health app and affiliated health plan websites, to help approximately 22 million commercial health plan members navigate their healthcare. This digital tool uses conversational technology to provide personalized guidance on coverage, benefits, and care, aiming to simplify the healthcare journey. The Virtual Assistant assists members in tasks such as checking claims, paying bills, finding in-network providers, and understanding costs, with planned expansion to Medicare members in 2026.

feed.title: WEC Energy Group Sets 2026 Executive Performance Measures
feed.summary: WEC Energy Group has announced its 2026 Executive Performance Measures, focusing on financial and operational achievements for its Short-Term Performance Plan. Executive incentive awards will be influenced by earnings per share, cash flow, and social performance areas. Long-term awards will be tied to total shareholder return and return on equity, with adjustments based on the stock price to earnings ratio, aligning executive interests with shareholder value.

feed.title: Financials Lead While Real Estate And Bitcoin Diverge
feed.summary: US financial shares saw gains, while the real estate sector presented a mixed performance, and Bitcoin experienced a notable decline. Major business deals and significant regulatory changes were key drivers behind these divergent movements across the markets. Investor confidence in financials appears strong, even with rising Treasury yields, while real estate shows sensitivity to interest rate changes and digital assets face ongoing uncertainty.

feed.title: Align Technology stock gains as competitor raises prices By Investing.com
feed.summary: Align Technology (NASDAQ:ALGN) stock rose after Stifel reiterated a Buy rating and $200 price target, noting a competitor's upcoming 8-9% price increase. This move by competitor Angelalign, particularly with Align's pending zero refinement plan priced at $850, could strengthen Align's market position. The news follows Align Technology's strong Q3 2025 results, which exceeded revenue and EPS expectations.

feed.title: Wall Street Processes Netflix-WB Deal: WBD Stock Up Slightly, Paramount And Netflix Shares Slump
feed.summary: Wall Street has begun reacting to Netflix's $82.7 billion acquisition of Warner Bros., with Netflix stock falling 3% and Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) stock rising 5%. Paramount has been the biggest loser, with its shares falling 8%. Analysts have raised concerns about regulatory scrutiny, Netflix's declining engagement, and the long-term commitment to theatrical releases.

feed.title: HALPER SADEH LLC ENCOURAGES THE WALT DISNEY COMPANY SHAREHOLDERS TO CONTACT THE FIRM TO DISCUSS THEIR RIGHTS
feed.summary: Halper Sadeh LLC, an investor rights law firm, is investigating potential breaches of fiduciary duties by certain officers and directors of The Walt Disney Company (NYSE: DIS). The firm encourages current long-term shareholders of Walt Disney to contact them to discuss their legal rights, which may include seeking corporate governance reforms, the return of funds to the company, or financial incentives. Halper Sadeh LLC handles these actions on a contingent fee basis, emphasizing that shareholder involvement can enhance corporate transparency and value.

feed.title: Chevron sets 2026 capex at $18–$19 billion, prioritizes high-return upstream growth
feed.summary: Chevron announced an organic capital expenditure range of $18–$19 billion for 2026, with a focus on disciplined spending, efficiency improvements, and high-return upstream development. The company plans to allocate approximately $17 billion to upstream activities, with over half of the total organic capex directed towards the United States for shale and tight oil developments. This strategy aims to expand cash flow and earnings while maintaining strategic flexibility and delivering superior shareholder returns.

feed.title: Is American Tower Now a Value Opportunity After Its Recent Share Price Slump?
feed.summary: American Tower (NYSE: AMT) has experienced an 11.6% share price decline over the last year, leading investors to question if it's a value opportunity. Despite the slump, Simply Wall St's analysis, including a Discounted Cash Flow model and Price-to-Earnings ratio, indicates the stock is currently undervalued. The company's valuation scores a 6/6, suggesting it is undervalued across all metrics tracked.

feed.title: A Closer Look at Amgen (AMGN) Valuation After Its 12-Month 28% Share Price Gain
feed.summary: Amgen (AMGN) has seen a significant 28% share price increase over the last 12 months, leading to questions about its current valuation. While the popular narrative suggests the stock is 5.4% overvalued compared to an estimated fair value of $322.88, its P/E ratio of 26.1 times earnings appears cheaper than the peer average. The article highlights Amgen's robust pipeline, particularly in personalized therapies, as a key factor supporting its future growth, despite potential challenges from drug pricing pressure and biosimilar competition.

feed.title: JPMorgan Chase & Co. Lowers Arbor Realty Trust (NYSE:ABR) Price Target to $9.00
feed.summary: JPMorgan Chase & Co. has reduced its price target for Arbor Realty Trust (NYSE:ABR) to $9.00 from $10.00, maintaining an "underweight" rating, which suggests a minimal downside from the previous closing price. This adjustment comes amidst mixed analyst sentiments, with a consensus "Reduce" rating and a target price of $10.75 across several firms. CEO Ivan Kaufman recently increased his stake in the company by purchasing 29,000 shares at $8.34.

feed.title: Constellation Reaches Resolution With U.S. Department of Justice for Calpine Transaction
feed.summary: Constellation (Nasdaq: CEG) announced it has reached a resolution with the U.S. Department of Justice (DOJ) on the conditions needed to complete the Calpine Corporation acquisition, marking the final regulatory clearance. The agreement involves divesting specific natural gas-fired generating assets in the Mid-Atlantic and Texas regions. This resolution paves the way for the closing of the transaction and integrating Calpine into Constellation's operations.

feed.title: CoStar Agrees to Shift Copyright Suit to Zillow’s Seattle Home
feed.summary: A federal court has approved CoStar Group Inc.'s request to move its copyright infringement lawsuit against Zillow Group Inc. from New York to Seattle, Zillow's home city. CoStar stated it did not oppose the transfer due to new information from Zillow, indicating that the case could be more efficiently handled in Seattle, citing newly identified Seattle-based witnesses and CoStar's own litigation experience in the Ninth Circuit. This move suggests a strategic decision by CoStar based on practical advantages.

feed.title: Is Regeneron Still Attractive After Recent Pipeline Milestones and Valuation Upside Signals?
feed.summary: Regeneron Pharmaceuticals (REGN) has seen recent stock volatility but shows a 51.7% gain over five years, driven by pipeline expansions and regulatory milestones in eye disease and immunology. Despite competitive pressures and drug pricing debates, Simply Wall St's valuation framework rates Regeneron as 5 out of 6, indicating potential undervaluation. Both Discounted Cash Flow and Price-to-Earnings analyses suggest the stock is currently undervalued, with estimated intrinsic value significantly higher than its current market price.

feed.title: Duke Energy to reimburse Creedmoor residents for damages after Thanksgiving week outages
feed.summary: Duke Energy announced it will reimburse Creedmoor customers for damages like those to HVAC systems and appliances, caused by an equipment failure during a power outage the day before Thanksgiving. This decision comes after a WRAL investigation into the incident, where two sagging power lines affected around 2,300 customers. Customers need to fill out an online claim form to qualify for reimbursement, and Duke Energy must verify their home was connected to the involved lines.

feed.title: Halliburton, McDermott among Houston companies adding new board members (free to read)
feed.summary: Four Houston-area energy companies, including Halliburton and McDermott, have appointed new members to their boards of directors. This includes one company that recently relocated to Houston.

feed.title: Teledyne FLIR wins U.S. Marine Corps contract to supply Rogue 1 loitering munition systems
feed.summary: Teledyne FLIR Defense has secured a $42.5 million contract from the U.S. Marine Corps for Delivery Order 3 of the Organic Precision Fires Light programme. The company will deliver over 600 Rogue 1 loitering munition systems, including ground control stations and training kits, starting this summer. These systems provide rifle squads and platoons with a man-packable, organic, loitering precision strike capability for engaging targets beyond line of sight.

feed.title: Hormel Sees Q4 Sales Slip As 2025 Profitability Goal Falls Short
feed.summary: Hormel Foods Corp. reported a significant slip in Q4 sales and profitability for fiscal year 2025, falling short of earnings goals due to a challenging cost environment, non-cash charges, and higher employee expenses. The company posted a net loss of $56 million in Q4 2025 compared to a net income of $220 million in Q4 2024, with full-year net income down to $477 million from $805 million. Hormel projects fiscal 2026 sales to be between $12.2 billion and $12.5 billion.

feed.title: Netflix Stock Falls. Wall Street Is Worried About Warner Deal’s High Price and Debt.
feed.summary: Netflix's stock has fallen as Wall Street expresses concerns over the company's acquisition of Warner Bros. Discovery. The deal, valued at nearly $83 billion, is seen as having a high price tag and involves Netflix taking on substantial debt. Despite Netflix framing it as a transformative deal, investors are worried about the financial implications.

feed.title: Why Is Qualcomm (QCOM) Up 0.7% Since Last Earnings Report?
feed.summary: Qualcomm (QCOM) shares have increased by 0.7% since its last earnings report, outperforming the S&P 500. The company reported strong Q4 fiscal 2025 results, with adjusted earnings and revenues surpassing estimates, driven by growth in IoT and automotive businesses. While estimates for QCOM have trended upward, the stock currently holds a Zacks Rank #3 (Hold), suggesting an in-line return expected in the coming months.

feed.title: Why Is Devon Energy (DVN) Up 16.3% Since Last Earnings Report?
feed.summary: Devon Energy's (DVN) shares have risen 16.3% since its last earnings report, outperforming the S&P 500. The company reported strong Q3 2025 results, beating EPS and revenue estimates, driven by increased production volumes. Despite a recent downward trend in estimate revisions, DVN currently holds a Zacks Rank #3 (Hold) with strong VGM scores.

feed.title: Why Is Cencora (COR) Down 6.3% Since Last Earnings Report?
feed.summary: Cencora (COR) shares have fallen 6.3% since its last earnings report, underperforming the S&P 500, prompting questions about its future stock performance. The company reported strong Q4 fiscal 2025 results, beating earnings and revenue estimates, alongside robust full-year growth and an improved gross margin. Cencora also issued optimistic fiscal 2026 guidance, yet analysts' estimates have shown a downward trend since the earnings release.

feed.title: Why Is Zimmer (ZBH) Up 5.3% Since Last Earnings Report?
feed.summary: Zimmer Biomet (ZBH) saw its shares rise 5.3% since its last earnings report, outperforming the S&P 500. The company reported Q3 2025 adjusted earnings of $1.90 per share, beating estimates, despite revenues of $2.00 billion missing the Zacks Consensus Estimate. Zimmer also updated its 2025 financial guidance, narrowing its constant currency revenue growth expectations.

feed.title: Why Is Trimble (TRMB) Up 4.4% Since Last Earnings Report?
feed.summary: Trimble's (TRMB) shares have risen 4.4% since its last earnings report, outperforming the S&P 500, driven by strong Q3 2025 results that beat estimates. The company saw revenue and earnings per share increase year-over-year, largely due to organic growth in its AECO and Field Systems segments. Trimble provided a positive outlook, anticipating continued revenue and earnings growth for the upcoming quarter and full year 2025, and into 2026 and 2027.

feed.title: Why Is McKesson (MCK) Down 5.2% Since Last Earnings Report?
feed.summary: McKesson (MCK) shares have dropped 5.2% since its last earnings report, underperforming the S&P 500, despite beating EPS estimates for Q2 fiscal 2026. The company's revenues missed expectations, though they grew year-over-year, and McKesson has raised its fiscal 2026 EPS guidance. Estimates for the stock have been broadly trending downward in the past month.

feed.title: Why Is Host Hotels (HST) Down 0.9% Since Last Earnings Report?
feed.summary: Host Hotels & Resorts (HST) saw its shares decrease by 0.9% since its last earnings report, underperforming the S&P 500. Despite this, the company reported third-quarter adjusted FFO per share of 35 cents, surpassing the Zacks Consensus Estimate, and raised its outlook for 2025 AFFO per share due to strong revenues and comparable hotel RevPAR growth. The article also touches on the company's Q3 financial details, balance sheet position, capital expenditure, and 2025 outlook, along with a positive Zacks Rank #2 (Buy) rating.

feed.title: Edwards Lifesciences (EW): Stifel Raises Price Target to $100
feed.summary: Stifel analyst Rick Wise has maintained a 'Buy' rating on Edwards Lifesciences (EW) and increased the price target from $95.00 to $100.00, indicating a positive outlook for the company. This follows a trend of positive adjustments from other analysts, reinforcing investor confidence. Edwards Lifesciences, a leader in medical devices for structural heart disease, derives significant sales from outside the US.
